title: Rich Molten Thai Shrimp Gaeng Curry
description: A funky, molten, one-pot Thai dish featuring ready rolled shortcrust pastry and shio-koji-cure, this low-calorie-friendly gaeng curry is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the shortcrust pastry and place it in a 9-inch round baking dish.
In a large skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium-high heat and cook the shrimp until pink and fully cooked.
In the same skillet, add the shio-koji, Thai red curry paste, fish sauce, and brown sugar, and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Pour the curry mixture into the baking dish with the pastry.
Fold the pastry over the filling to form a crust.
Bake the gaeng curry for 20 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
Serve the curry hot, garnished with fresh cilantro.
